steps.title: Summarize what the passage actually does
steps.content: The passage opens by noting that few animals spit, then reports that a gray seal was observed spitting at an eagle — a behavior never seen before in seals — and closes with a biologist's hypotheses about why.

steps.title: Identify the unifying topic
steps.content: Every sentence centers on this newly observed seal behavior: setup, the sighting, the novelty, and possible explanations. The main topic is a behavior of gray seals that had not been previously documented.

steps.title: Test each choice against the whole passage
steps.content:
• Choice A — Bird-watching in southern England: the bird-watcher and location are setup details, not the topic; the passage is about the seal, not the hobby. ✗
  • Choice B — A previously unseen behavior of gray seals: matches the focus that runs through every sentence. ✔
  • Choice C — How white-tailed eagles defend their territory against other predators: the eagle is the target of the spitting, not the subject; the passage never discusses eagle defense. ✗
  • Choice D — Differences between gray seals and white-tailed eagles: no comparison of the two species is made; this misreads the passage. ✗
That is the correct answer.
